title: Chapter 1: Introduction & Course Orientation
description: An overview of the course structure, objectives, resources, and tools, along with an initial assessment of students' prior knowledge.

title: Chapter 2: Software Engineering & SDLC Overview
description: Discussion of fundamental software development lifecycle (SDLC) models such as Waterfall, Agile, and Spiral, including their applications and suitability.

title: Chapter 3: Requirements Engineering
description: Introduction to eliciting, analyzing, and documenting software requirements using industry-standard techniques to ensure clear and thorough specifications.

title: Chapter 4: Requirements Modeling & Visual Tools
description: Utilizing UML and other visual modeling tools to represent requirements effectively through diagrams and models.

title: Chapter 5: Software Architecture & Design Principles
description: Designing modular, scalable software architectures using established design principles and patterns.

title: Chapter 6: Design Patterns in Practice
description: Analyzing and applying common design patterns to solve software design problems and improve maintainability.

title: Chapter 7: Development Environments & Version Control
description: Using integrated development environments (IDEs) and version control systems like Git to facilitate efficient development workflows.

title: Chapter 8: Implementation Techniques & Debugging
description: Developing quality code with best practices and utilizing debugging tools to identify and resolve issues effectively.

title: Chapter 9: Testing Methodologies: Unit, Integration, System
description: Planning and executing various levels of testing to ensure software quality using frameworks such as JUnit and pytest.

title: Chapter 10: Automation & Test Management Tools
description: Leveraging automation tools like Jenkins and Selenium to streamline testing processes and manage test executions.

title: Chapter 11: Team Collaboration & Agile Practices
description: Collaborating effectively within teams using Agile methodologies and tools such as Jira and Trello.

title: Chapter 12: Project Management & Effective Communication
description: Managing project scope, risks, and documentation while communicating progress clearly within development teams.

title: Chapter 13: Professional Ethics & Standards
description: Analyzing ethical considerations and applying ACM/IEEE codes of ethics to real-world scenarios in software engineering.

title: Chapter 14: Course Project Presentations & Wrap-up
description: Presenting final group projects, synthesizing learning outcomes, and receiving feedback from peers and instructors.
